title: Mastermyne Crinum Operations Pty Ltd — Public safety
investigation_objective: Mastermyne Crinum Operations Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of the Metarock Group, was found guilty by jury of industrial manslaughter under Queensland's Coal Mining Safety and Health Act 1999 - the first such conviction since the offence took effect in 2020 - following the death of underground miner Graham Dawson in a roof collapse at the Crinum coal mine on 14 September 2021. Resources Safety and Health Queensland found the company's strata control systems were inadequate. The District Court of Queensland imposed a $7 million fine plus costs, Australia's largest-ever work health and safety penalty; the company has lodged a notice of appeal.
scope: First-ever conviction for industrial manslaughter under Queensland's Coal Mining Safety and Health Act 1999; Fined $7 million plus costs, described as Australia's largest-ever work health and safety penalty; Regulator found the company's strata control systems were inadequate; Company has lodged a notice of appeal against the conviction
